rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Courthouse Ventures Inc., Xiaowen Li, Yong Liang, Joseph Giraudo, Beverly Giraudo, Young Liang Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff James D. Banks To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 20, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF JAMES D. BANKS TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS; AND FOR AN AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRANTED. Plaintiff to serve complete verified responses without objections and responsive documents within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant's request for sanctions against the Plaintiff is GRANTED in the amount of $510.00, payment to be made within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 20, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT CHONG HONG'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 30, 2021. By no later than September 10, 2021 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to the following: (1) page and line number in the 5th Amended Complaint alleging cause of action for conversion; (2) in a table format for each cause of action by each Plaintiff (accounting, misrepresentation, quantum meruit, breach of oral contract, breach of guarantee) list each element of each cause of action and facts (not conclusions) Plaintiffs can allege in good faith to satisfy each element as to each defendant, e.g. Cause of action for breach of written contract (column 1: element; column 2: facts as to Defendant X; facts as to defendant Y, etc.). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 20, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT JOHN PATTERSON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that HA rules and requirements are not applicable to the tenancy at bar notwithstanding the provisions of the lease.
